headline: Are ServiceNow’s (NOW) Rejected Governance Changes Overshadowing Its Expanding AI Partnership Narrative?
summary: In May 2026, ServiceNow held its annual general meeting, where shareholders rejected a proposal from John Chevedden to allow action by written consent. headline: Retail investors fuel record buying in US software ETF as AI trade broadens
summary: The largest exchange-traded fund (ETF) tracking U.S. software stocks clocked the biggest single-day buy-in by retail investors ‌on record on Monday, data from Vanda Research showed. Software ‌stocks have largely recovered their losses from earlier this year when fears of ​industry-wide disruptions due to the rise of AI gripped the sector, though volatility still persists. Mom-and-pop traders bought stock worth a net $46 million in the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF on Monday, surpassing ‌the previous single-day ⁠record by roughly 40%, as per Vanda.

headline: Software stocks whipsaw amid AI fears, as Anthropic IPO looms
summary: The software sector is currently caught in a tug-of-war between profound optimism and existential dread over the future of artificial intelligence. headline: ServiceNow (NOW) Shares Skyrocket, What You Need To Know
summary: Shares of enterprise workflow automation company ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) jumped 9.4% in the afternoon session after it continued to rally extending double digit gains from the previous session, which was driven by two forces: Snowflake's blowout Q1 results showing enterprise AI adoption is accelerating rather than disrupting software demand, and Dell's confirmation that enterprises are committing to full-stack AI infrastructure at a scale nobody had modelled.
